headline: Procurement fraud costs Malaysia RM15 billion annually says TI
context: Transparency International Malaysia estimated procurement fraud costs the federal government RM15 billion per year, roughly 4.5% of total government expenditure.

cards.big: RM15 billion lost to procurement fraud every year — enough to abolish tolls nationwide
cards.sub: TI Malaysia says 4.5% of government spending disappears into corrupt practices

cards.big: Government Procurement Act 2024 was supposed to mandate e-procurement by January 2026
cards.sub: Implementation delayed twice — manual processes still dominate 60% of agencies

cards.big: E-procurement in South Korea reduced procurement costs by 22% within 3 years
cards.sub: Malaysia's delay costs roughly RM3.3 billion annually in preventable losses

cards.big: Only 4 out of 28 ministries publish awarded contracts on their websites
cards.sub: Without transparency, citizens cannot verify if procurement rules were followed

cards.big: Every ringgit stolen through procurement is a ringgit stolen from hospitals, schools, and roads
Should all government contracts above RM1 million be published in a central database?
